title: WatchAct: A Benchmark for Behavior-Grounded Robot Manipulation
abstract: arXiv:2606.26443v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: A robot working alongside people must reason about what they have done, in what order, and with what intent. Video carries the spatial layouts, object histories, and gestures that language leaves underspecified, yet today's manipulation benchmarks pair an instruction with a single current image, offering no way to evaluate reasoning over observed human behavior. We introduce WatchAct, a benchmark for robot manipulation grounded in observed human behavior. Each instance pairs a real-world human-action video and a language instruction with an aligned simulator scene and an executable LIBERO task, enabling scalable and reproducible evaluation. WatchAct comprises 3,000 long-horizon instances across 14 tasks in four capability domains drawn from the cognitive demands of watching another agent: parsing events (Event Grounding), recovering procedural structure (Procedural Reasoning), inferring unstated intent (Implicit Intent Inference), and

Title: WatchAct: A Benchmark for Behavior-Grounded Robot Manipulation
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26443v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: A robot working alongside people must reason about what they have done, in what order, and with what intent. Video carries the spatial layouts, object histories, and gestures that language leaves underspecified, yet today's manipulation benchmarks pair an instruction with a single current image, offering no way to evaluate reasoning over observed human behavior. We introduce WatchAct, a benchmark for robot manipulation grounded in observed human behavior. Each instance pairs a real-world human-action video and a language instruction with an aligned simulator scene and an executable LIBERO task, enabling scalable and reproducible evaluation. WatchAct comprises 3,000 long-horizon instances across 14 tasks in four capability domains drawn from the cognitive demands of watching another agent: parsing events (Event Grounding), recovering procedural structure (Procedural Reasoning), inferring unstated intent (Implicit Intent Inference), and
translated_title: WatchAct：行为驱动型机器人操作的基准测试
translated_abstract: 与人类并肩工作的机器人必须理解人类做了什么、以何种顺序、以及出于何种意图。视频包含了空间布局、物体历史和手势等语言未能明确表达的信息，但当今的操作基准测试通常仅将指令与单张当前图像配对，无法评估机器人对观察到的行为进行推理的能力。我们提出了WatchAct，一个基于观察人类行为的机器人操作基准测试。每个实例将真实世界的人类动作视频和语言指令与对齐的模拟器场景及可执行的LIBERO任务配对，支持可扩展且可重复的评估。WatchAct包含3000个长时域实例，覆盖四个能力领域的14项任务，这些任务源自观察其他智能体时的认知需求：解析事件（事件基础）、恢复程序结构（程序推理）、推断未明示的意图（隐式意图推理）。
tldr: 该论文提出了一个基于人类行为视频和语言指令的机器人操作基准测试WatchAct，包含3000个长时域实例，覆盖事件解析、程序推理和隐式意图推理等能力，以评估机器人对观察到的行为进行推理的能力。
en_tdlr: The paper introduces WatchAct, a benchmark for robot manipulation grounded in observed human behavior, comprising 3,000 long-horizon instances across event grounding, procedural reasoning, and implicit intent inference capabilities to evaluate robot reasoning over observed actions.
